integration_note: Phase 7 was re-integrated against the OC-ALL-01 governed schemas after main was merged. The dashboard now consumes contracts/schemas/dashboard_truth_projection.schema.json (preferred) or contracts/schemas/operational_closure_bundle.schema.json (fallback). The OC layer's own self-reported drift (freshness_status, alignment_status, dashboard_alignment, fast_trust_gate_sufficiency, current_bottleneck.category=unknown, next_work_item.selection_status=unknown) maps onto the dashboard's stale_proof / conflict_proof / ambiguous fail-closed states.

current_repo_state.artifacts/operational_closure/*: absent in this branch (no static published path yet)

behavior_when_oc_unavailable.api: /api/oc-bottleneck returns state=unavailable + reason='OC artifacts not present: <projection>, <bundle>'
behavior_when_oc_unavailable.ui: Diagnostics tab Current Bottleneck panel surfaces 'OC bottleneck: unavailable — <reason>' instead of fabricating a card
behavior_when_oc_unavailable.core_dashboard_behavior: Top 3 / Trust Pulse / Graph all continue to operate; OC absence is reported, not propagated as a critical fail-closed across the cockpit

abuse_cases.actual: loadOcBottleneck returns state='stale_proof' when projection.freshness_status='stale'; UI shows 'OC bottleneck: stale_proof — <reason>'; warnings list freshness_status=stale
loadOcBottleneck returns state='conflict_proof' when projection.alignment_status in {drifted, corrupt, missing} or bundle.dashboard_alignment in same set
loadOcBottleneck returns state='ambiguous' when either status or category is 'unknown', or when next_work_item.selection_status='unknown'

abuse_cases.expected: warnings panel surfaces fast_trust_gate_sufficiency / dashboard_alignment drift
abuse_cases.actual: card.warnings includes 'fast_trust_gate_sufficiency=insufficient' / 'dashboard_alignment=drifted' so the operator sees the OC layer's self-doubt without needing to read evidence_refs

abuse_cases.expected: panel notes that Top 3 and OC are distinct surfaces; no auto-merge
abuse_cases.actual: Diagnostics panel adds explanatory text 'Top 3 (priority) and OC bottleneck are distinct surfaces; mismatches are reported here without a tie-break'
loadOcBottleneck returns state='unavailable' when no projection AND no bundle file is present on disk; sources lists both expected paths plus their schemas
isProjection enforces required enum values for current_status / bottleneck_category / freshness_status / alignment_status and non-empty reason_code; mismatches return invalid_schema
Loader prefers projection (more direct dashboard projection); only falls back to bundle when projection is absent. Test 'prefers projection over bundle when both are present' confirms.
